chapter_title.en: Chapter 61 – The Fast of (the day of) Arafat (9<sup>th</sup> Zil Hijja), and Ashoura (10<sup>th</sup> Muharram)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/360)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/360)
text: ا لَا تَصُمْ فِي يَوْمِ عَاشُورَاءَ وَ لَا عَرَفَةَ بِمَكَّةَ وَ لَا فِي الْمَدِينَةِ وَ لَا فِي وَطَنِكَ وَ لَا فِي مِصْرٍ مِنَ الْأَمْصَارِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Nuh Bin Shuayb Al Neyshapouri, fom Yaseen Al Zareer, from Hareyz, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> or Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> both having said: ‘Do not Fast during the day of Ashura (10<sup>th</sup> Muharram), nor Arafat (9<sup>th</sup> Zil-Hijja), neither in Makkah, nor in Al-Medina, nor in your hometown, nor in a city from the cities (during these two days)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Nuh ibn Shu’ayb al-Naysaburiy, from Yasin al-Darir from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, and abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, have said, ‘You must not fast on the day of ‘Ashura’ (tenth of Muharram) and on the day of ‘Arafah in Makkah, in al-Madinah, in your homeland or in any other city.’”
